body.text: I am writing to express my interest in the Principal Engineer role at Studio Lacuna. Having followed the studio's work on computational urbanism for the past three years, I believe the intersection of your research ambitions and my technical background represents an unusually strong alignment.

Over the past four years at Forma Studio, I have led the design and delivery of parametric design tooling used on live projects exceeding two million square feet. The work required me to operate simultaneously as a software engineer, a researcher, and a communicator — writing production TypeScript on Monday, co-authoring an ACADIA paper on Tuesday, and presenting to a client board on Wednesday. I am equally comfortable in all three registers.

What draws me specifically to Studio Lacuna is the seriousness with which the practice treats the relationship between computation and material culture. My master's research at Harvard GSD — generative structural optimisation from voxel fields — was motivated by exactly this question, and I have never fully left it. I would welcome the opportunity to return to a research context where that question is central rather than peripheral.

I would be glad to discuss the role further at your convenience. I am available for a call or a visit to Copenhagen at short notice and can provide portfolio materials, code samples, or references on request.
